text:
Once upon a time, in a small mountain village nestled between towering pine trees, there lived a wise old owl named Oliver.

Ollie knew everything about his woodland. He knew the habits of rabbits who liked mint leaves, the calls of owls that hinged like flags, and the secret routes of shy moths. Every morning at dawn he'd flap gracefully around his treehouse, catching mosquitoes and listening to the chatter of birds.

One fine autumn day, a young squirrel named Felix hopped into Oliver's treehouse. Felix was bright-eyed and full of questions.

"Aren't you ever bored?" Felix asked.

"With you? Never!" replied Oliver with a chuckle. "You just provide endless entertainment. Why don't you ever visit?"

"Sometimes I feel too big," Felix confided.

Oliver thought for a moment. "Well, there used to be a magical path where you could glide?"
